title: Hoops Hero: George Wilson's Basketball Journey
story: Slam dunk! George Wilson, a towering basketball center, entered the world in Meridian, Mississippi on May 9, 1942. His talent soared as he clinched an NCAA Championship with the University of Cincinnati in 1962. Wilson's golden moment came in 1964 when he helped the USA win Olympic gold. He then bounced through a pro career with teams like the Cincinnati Royals and Chicago Bulls.

title: Rock Star's Birthday Bash
story: Grab your guitar and get ready to rock! Tom Petersson, the talented bassist for the band Cheap Trick, was born on May 9, 1950. title: Simple Plan's Lead Singer Rocks Into Life
story: Pierre Bouvier, the voice behind hit songs like 'Perfect' and 'Welcome To My Life', burst onto the scene in 1979. title: Prince of the Diamond: Fielder's Grand Slam
story: Batter up! Prince Fielder swung into the world on May 9, 1984. This future baseball star would become a powerhouse first baseman, smashing home runs and earning All-Star status six times. title: Cheers to the Beer Pump Handle!
story: Thirsty pub-goers rejoiced in 1785 when Joseph Bramah, a clever British inventor, patented the beer pump handle on May 9. This nifty device made pouring drinks much easier and faster. Before Bramah's invention, bartenders had to manually lift heavy barrels or use complicated systems to serve beer.

bonus_fact: Bramah also invented an improved toilet flush and a pick-proof lock.
